campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule (Ordinance 2326) and moved forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Extension of Ordinance 2326 (temporary local rule)
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant applications for the Hamilton Avenue overcross project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents could speak about a proposed new city rule. After hearing from the public, the council introduced a new ordinance (city law) and decided to skip the usual first formal reading so they can vote on approving it at their next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on new ordinance
New city rule introduced and first reading waived to speed up approval
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next council meeting
Fast-tracked process allows adoption without standard preliminary step

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several previous sessions in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was mostly administrative, focusing on finalizing records from past meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The City Council met to approve several important agreements and projects. They discussed updates to the airport's baggage handling system, plans to protect affordable housing if subsidies end, improvements to the city's storm sewer system, and a family camp project funded by FEMA disaster relief.
keyTopics: Airport baggage conveyor system upgrade contract
Affordable housing protections if federal subsidies run out
Storm sewer system planning and improvements
FEMA-funded family camp project

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what construction and improvement projects will get funded. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Budget approval for city construction and infrastructure projects in 2026-27
Teen Month proclamation recognizing youth contributions
National Volunteer Week recognition for community volunteers
Capital improvement priorities for the city

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and highlighted two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These special recognitions aim to celebrate the city's diverse community and promote fair housing practices for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community diversity celebration
Housing rights and anti-discrimination efforts

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting focused on helping residents understand how the city spends money through a tool called OpenGov, which shows financial details publicly. The council also discussed rules for lobbyists—people who try to influence city decisions—requiring them to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demonstration for public budget access
New lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under City code
Community engagement workshop on city spending and budgets
Administrative rules for those attempting to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to take legal action, you have 90 days from when the decision is final to file a lawsuit.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal disclosure requirements for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 governs lawsuit timelines

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's five-year housing and community development plan, review work requests from individual council members, and consider changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Update to 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
Changes to how council member vacancies are handled
New work requests from individual council members
Scheduling and administrative meeting updates

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking the city to officially recognize it as a more historically significant building, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and upgrading the structure to meet earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus building space for seismic improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown Commercial zone development incentives
